fix: Feed tab shows 'Link Connector' for already-connected agents + auto-fetch messages

Bug 1 (agents_sprint.py): GET /channel-messages returned has_connectors=False
for agents whose ConnectorAgent rows were missing (older agents or transient
creation failure). Add self-heal: on empty ConnectorAgent query, read
agent.config.connector_ids and idempotently create the missing rows. Handles
race conditions via IntegrityError catch (re-queries after rollback) and clears
connector_ids on general commit failure so the response stays accurate.

Bug 2 (AgentMessagesTab.tsx): Feed tab showed empty waiting state even when
Discord was connected because the local DB had no cached messages. Add a
one-shot backfill mutation (POST /channel-messages/backfill) that fires on
mount when has_connectors=True but messages=0. Shows 'Fetching latest messages
from Discord...' while in-flight. Uses a ref to prevent repeated calls.

Also imports IntegrityError from sqlalchemy.exc for precise exception handling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 3: webkit-jsi JS runtime for YouTube anti-throttle

Bundle yt-dlp-apple-webkit-jsi plugin for YouTube signature deobfuscation.
The plugin uses ctypes → libobjc → WKWebView → JavaScriptCore on iOS
to evaluate YouTube's obfuscated JS, preventing download throttling.

CI:
- pip install yt-dlp-apple-webkit-jsi in both build jobs
- Verify webkit_jsi.py present in packages and IPA bundle
- Verify _ctypes.framework present in Frameworks/

Python:
- check_js_runtime() diagnostic: tests ctypes import, plugin install,
  and availability using plugin's own IS_AVAIL + DarwinMinVer logic

Swift:
- checkJsRuntime method channel handler (runWithResult for GIL safety)

Flutter:
- checkJsRuntime() in LibraryManager
- _JsRuntimeSection in settings: shows ctypes, plugin, platform status
- Shows 'anti-throttle active' when all checks pass on iOS

Note: _ctypes.so is already in BeeWare Python-Apple-support 3.13-b13
and gets auto-converted to .framework by post_build.sh. No build
pipeline changes needed for the C extension itself.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix --version in release builds: use OSCAN_VERSION env var

build.rs now checks OSCAN_VERSION env var first, falls back to git
describe. The release workflow sets OSCAN_VERSION from the prepare
job's tag output, ensuring the binary always reports the correct
release version (not the previous tag).

Also added cargo:rerun-if-env-changed=OSCAN_VERSION so Cargo
correctly rebuilds when the env var changes.

commit.message:
Ⓜ feat: add configurable agent marker for send and edit messages (#40)

Add agentMarker parameter to sendMessage and editMessage actions that
prepends a configurable prefix to message content. Supports per-call
parameter override and TEAMS_AGENT_MARKER env var fallback.

Key behaviors:
- Parameter takes precedence over env var
- Empty string explicitly disables the marker
- Marker is not prepended to empty content
- For editMessage, marker is applied before reply guard annotation

Closes #25
Closes #29

commit.message:
Add Greens in Regulation (GIR) stat throughout the app

GIR formula: a hole is GIR when (score - putts) <= (par - 2)
  e.g. Par 4, score 5, 3 putts → 5-3=2 ≤ 4-2=2 → GIR ✓

Changes:
- calcGIR() helper computes hits, total and pct for a set of holes
- History cards: show GIR% per round alongside putts
- Stats summary: two new cards — GIR Overall % and GIR Last 5 (rolling avg)
- Stats charts: new GIR % Per Round line chart (last 10 rounds, 0-100% Y axis)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(theme): fix dark/light toggle — default to dark, consistent across screens (#510)

Fixes #491

## Changes
- Changed default theme preference from SYSTEM to DARK in UserPreferences.kt
- Updated MainActivity initial value to match DARK default
- Replaced hardcoded colors in SettingsScreen with MaterialTheme.colorScheme:
  - Color.White → MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurface
  - Color(0xFF9E9E9E) → MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant
  - Ensures theme changes properly affect all screens

## Testing
- App now defaults to dark theme on first install
- Theme toggle works consistently across all screens
- No hardcoded light colors preventing theme changes

commit.message:
feat: update StreamingService for multi-protocol streaming

Replace lazy EncoderBridge with per-session nullable bridge created via
ProtocolAwareBridgeFactory. Add buildConnectionParams() to construct
typed Rtmp/Srt params from EndpointProfile. Add buildEncoderConfig()
to snapshot encoder settings from SettingsRepository flows.

Validate codec/protocol compatibility before stream start (rejects AV1
over SRT). Bridge is released on stop and recreated for each session.

feat: add codec-aware device capability queries

Update DeviceCapabilityQuery interface with codec parameter (default

commit.message:
Optimize full (and light/medium) index creation

Three independent optimizations, all in indexing.py.

Stage 1: replace per-run np.lexsort with the native Cython
intra_chunk_sort_run kernel in _build_full_descriptor_ooc().
Eliminates the np.arange allocation, the O(N log N) lexsort, and
two gather passes in favour of the GIL-free stable mergesort.

Stage 2: replace _merge_sorted_slices (concat + lexsort, O(N log N)
linear merge, GIL-free) inside _merge_run_pair().

Stage 3: rewrite _build_levels_descriptor_ooc() with a single-pass
strategy.  Previously the function issued one array[start:stop]
decompression call per segment across all levels (e.g. 9008 calls for
10M rows on a Mac M4: 8 chunk + 1000 block + 8000 subblock).  The new
code decompresses each array chunk exactly once and computes all level
summaries in a single vectorized pass via _fill_summaries_from_2d(),
falling back to the original per-segment path only when segment sizes
do not divide the chunk length evenly.

Measured on 10M random float64:

  Mac mini M4 (chunks=1.25M, blocks=10K):
    light:  940 ms ->  431 ms  (2.2x)
    medium: 4696 ms -> 394 ms (11.9x)
    full:   9650 ms -> 1584 ms  (6.1x)

  AMD 7800X3D Linux (chunks=2M, blocks=20K):
    light:   603 ms -> 430 ms  (1.4x)
    medium: 2050 ms -> 395 ms  (5.2x)
    full:   6856 ms -> 1582 ms  (4.3x)

Query latency and index sizes are unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(client): add API error scenario and rate limiting tests (#158)

* Initial plan

* test(client): add API error scenario and rate limiting tests

- Add integration tests for 500 Internal Server Error retry
- Add integration tests for 502 Bad Gateway retry
- Add connection refused retry integration test
- Add concurrent API calls test
- Enhance ServerState with SetGlobalHTTPStatus and SetIntermittentFailure
  for configurable error injection across all endpoints
- Add tests for new mock server capabilities
- Optimize checkGlobalOverride to use RLock for fast-path (no overrides)"

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/joelp172/uptime-robot-operator/sessions/a1fea383-dbcd-4de5-8599-63ee76c8492e

Co-authored-by: joelp172 <51311693+joelp172@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove unreachable return statements after t.Fatal

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/joelp172/uptime-robot-operator/sessions/a1fea383-dbcd-4de5-8599-63ee76c8492e

Co-authored-by: joelp172 <51311693+joelp172@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(test): improve test quality from PR review feedback

- Merge duplicate 500/502 retry tests into table-driven TestDoWithRetry_RetryableStatusCodes
- Use t.Fatalf instead of t.Errorf for fatal precondition checks
- Add response status code assertion in concurrent requests test
- Add end-to-end TestDoWithRetry_IntermittentFailureWithTestServer using test server
- Simplify checkGlobalOverride locking (remove TOCTOU-prone read-lock fast path)
- Fix inaccurate comments (backoff timing, rate limiter, SetIntermittentFailure docs)

* fix(test): fail loudly when test fixture file is missing in serveJSONFile

Previously serveJSONFile silently returned {status: ok} when a
fixture file was not found, causing tests to pass while validating
nothing. Now returns HTTP 500 with a descriptive error so missing or
renamed fixtures are caught immediately.

* refactor(test): use RLock fast-path and release lock before I/O in checkGlobalOverride

Address PR review feedback: use RLock to avoid serialising normal
requests, copy override state under write lock then release before
writing the HTTP response. Remove stale doc comment referencing a
read-lock fast path that no longer matched the implementation. Add
test for missing fixture error path to cover codecov gap.

* fix(ci): make e2e deploy install cert-manager first

Use deploy-with-cert-manager in both e2e workflows so deploy no
longer fails cert-manager-check on fresh Kind clusters.

* ci(workflows): upgrade actions for node24 compatibility

Bump actions/github-script from v7 to v8 and actions/setup-go from
v5 to v6 across CI/e2e/release workflows to remove Node 20 deprecation
warnings on GitHub-hosted runners.

* fix(test): address PR review comments on override middleware and tests

- Set Content-Type: application/json in checkGlobalOverride responses
- Disable rate limiter in concurrent test for true concurrency coverage
- Fix misleading "after reset" comment to "after clearing the override"

* fix(e2e): use dynamic future date for once maintenancewindow

Replace hardcoded once interval startDate with a generated UTC future date
to keep the e2e spec valid over time and aligned with webhook validation.

* ci(e2e): increase go test timeout to 45 minutes

Raise the E2E suite timeout from 20m to 45m to prevent CI failures
caused by long-running API cleanup and reconciliation waits.

* fix(e2e): poll observedGeneration to reduce status flakes

Add a shared helper that waits for non-empty observedGeneration and use
it in maintenancewindow, account/contact, monitorgroup, and
slackintegration e2e assertions to avoid one-shot timing races.

* refactor(test): move status code into serveJSONFile to prevent silent fixture failures

Callers like handleCreateMaintenanceWindow and handleCreateMonitorGroup
wrote 201 before calling serveJSONFile, so a missing fixture would still
return 201 with a garbled body instead of a clear 500 error. Add a
statusCode parameter to serveJSONFile so headers are only written after
the fixture is successfully opened.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: joelp172 <51311693+joelp172@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Joel Pinder <joel.pinder@adarga.ai>

commit.message:
fix: download E4B on FLAGSHIP devices in onboarding (#201)

* fix: download E4B on FLAGSHIP devices in onboarding

OnboardingViewModel now injects HardwareProfileDetector and exposes
preferredGemmaModel (E4B on FLAGSHIP ≥10GB, E2B otherwise).

OnboardingScreen uses startPreferredDownload() and shows the actual
model name in the download button label and progress row.

Fixes S23 Ultra (11GB RAM, FLAGSHIP) always downloading E2B.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#195): apply AppCompat theme to RedirectUriReceiverActivity

AppAuth's RedirectUriReceiverActivity requires a Theme.AppCompat theme.
Our app uses a pure Material3/Compose theme so the activity crashed with
IllegalStateException on every OAuth redirect attempt, silently killing
the HuggingFace sign-in flow before the token exchange could happen.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: 시스템 상호간섭 파라미터 충돌 감지기 (레짐 분류기 ↔ 동적 손절 ↔ 포지션 생애주기) (#134)

* Initial plan

* feat: add system interference parameter conflict checker

Detects 4 types of cross-system parameter conflicts between
Market Regime Classifier, Dynamic Stop, and Position Lifecycle:

- [CRITICAL] buyingHalted signal not reaching Position Lifecycle ENTRY
- [HIGH]  DynamicStopRegime (3-type) vs MarketRegimeClassification (4-type) mismatch
- [HIGH]  gate1BreachThreshold hardcoded in lifecycle vs regime-adjusted value
- [MEDIUM] positionSizeLimitPct not propagated to either sub-engine

New files:
  src/types/interference.ts
  src/services/quant/systemInterferenceChecker.ts
  src/components/SystemInterferencePanel.tsx

Updated files:

fix(nav): add back navigation to Exercise Library screen (#498)

Adds TopAppBar with back button to ExerciseLibraryScreen so users
can navigate back from Profile > Exercise Library without getting stuck.

The screen now uses Scaffold with TopAppBar containing:
- Back arrow navigation icon
- Screen title (dynamic based on picker/library mode)
- Create exercise button in actions (non-picker mode only)

Both exercise_library and exercise_picker routes now pass onNavigateBack
callback to ExerciseLibraryRoute.

Closes #492

repository.description: MCP server for structured file patching operations

commit.message:
fix(sync): harden atomic file writes

Route internal/sync append-only updates and internal/hooks settings writes through atomic temp-file replacement so delayed Close() failures, temp-file collisions, and partial writes do not report success. Preserve target file modes, clean up temp files, and normalize empty append-only inputs so merges do not introduce stray blank lines.

Add regression coverage for Close() handling, temp-file cleanup, destination preservation, permission preservation, and empty append-only merges.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Manuel Martinez <manmartgarc@gmail.com>

commit.message:
test(usage-analysis): add unit tests for mergeUsageAnalysis, analyzeContextReferences, deriveConversationPatterns (#600)

Add 23 unit tests covering the three synchronous/pure-function layers of
usageAnalysis.ts: mergeUsageAnalysis (period accumulation, MCP tool merging,
model-switching stats, byKind context tracking), analyzeContextReferences (all
13 reference type patterns, accumulation, case-insensitivity), and
deriveConversationPatterns (0/1/multi-request session patterns). Tests use
inline object construction with no mocks or external dependencies.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Add mentor automation system with signup, login, and dynamic profile display

- Add mentor database table with tracks (english, backend), bio, rating
- Create mentor signup/login endpoints: POST /api/mentor/signup, POST /api/mentor/login
- Create mentor profile endpoints: GET /api/mentor/profile, PUT /api/mentor/profile
- Create public mentor listing: GET /api/mentors?track=english|backend
- Add mentor role selection page to auth.html with separate mentor signup form
- Mentor can select multiple tracks (English or Backend) during signup
- Replace hardcoded mentor cards with dynamic rendering from API
- Mentor cards display name, bio, tracks, rating from database
- Update homepage sections to fetch and display mentors by track
- Both English Coaching and Backend Interview Prep sections now pull from database

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
